Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails using search_emails with query "Raj" or "VPN" or "access roster" or "inactive staff" (or similar) to find Raj's report on the VPN access audit. Expected findings: Denise's request email (email_inject_vpn_001) asking Raj to pull the VPN access roster for inactive staff, sent April 24, and Raj's reply (email_inject_vpn_002) identifying three former staff with active VPN profiles: Rachel Kim (terminated June 30, 2025) with three VPN sessions on April 20, 21, and 22 from a Charlotte IP, Andre Washington (terminated December 2024), and Todd Jennings (terminated July 2024). Raj's reply details that Rachel Kim's credentials accessed the email gateway and QuickBooks during the April 21 session and Stripe admin portal during the April 22 session. Grace Yamamoto is CC'd on this email.
OE 2: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "VPN" or "access" or "Raj" or "inactive" (or similar), or browse #it-support (C008) using conversations_history with channel_id "C008", to find Raj's Slack messages about the access audit. Expected findings: Denise's initial request in #it-support (April 24) asking Raj to pull the VPN roster, Raj's reply confirming three inactive staff with active VPN profiles and Rachel Kim's sessions from a Charlotte IP, Raj's follow-up (April 27) reporting that Rachel Kim's credentials also accessed Stripe admin portal during the April 22 session, and Denise's reply (April 27) confirming she found a $500 unauthorized refund on a Jessica Kim appraisal charge.
OE 3: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "compliance" or "unauthorized" or "credential" (or similar), or browse #compliance-alerts (C004) using conversations_history with channel_id "C004", to find Denise's formal alert. Expected findings: Denise's alert posted April 27 stating unauthorized system access using a former employee's VPN credentials that were never revoked after termination in June 2025, the same credentials accessed QuickBooks (fraudulent vendor and bill created) and Stripe (unauthorized refund processed), and instructions not to process payments to Lakeview Compliance Consulting.
OE 4: List or search Stripe refunds using stripe_list_refunds (or similar) to find the unauthorized refund. Expected findings: refund re_Kq8RvTm3nWx5pL2d for 50000 cents ($500), status "succeeded", reason "requested_by_customer", on charge ch_zfM80jSh4gy7m8X5, created at timestamp 1776909600 (April 22, 2026 evening).
OE 5: Get the associated Stripe charge using stripe_get_charge with charge_id "ch_zfM80jSh4gy7m8X5" (or similar) to identify the borrower and loan. Expected findings: charge amount 50000 cents ($500), description "Appraisal fee -- Kim (LN-2025-00483)", customer cus_ah4uUEeIp6iY0kbg, refunded status true, amount_refunded 50000.
OE 6: Search the Mortgage LOS for the loan using mortgage_los_search_loans with query "LN-2025-00483" or "Kim" (or similar). Expected findings: loan LN-2025-00483 for borrower Jessica Kim, status "conditional_approval", assigned loan officer Natasha Okafor, assigned processor Elena Marchetti, lender AmeriHome Mortgage, property in Charlotte NC.
OE 7: Search emails using search_emails with query "refund" or "appraisal" or "Jessica Kim" or "Natasha" (or similar) to find the loan officer's denial. Expected findings: Denise's email (email_inject_refund_001) asking Natasha Okafor whether she authorized a $500 refund on the Jessica Kim appraisal fee for loan LN-2025-00483, and Natasha's reply (email_inject_refund_002) confirming she did not process any refund, the borrower has not contacted her, the loan is still in conditional approval, and she did not authorize a $500 refund. Elena Marchetti is CC'd on Natasha's reply.
OE 8: Look up Rachel Kim in the LOS staff records using mortgage_los_list_staff with active_only set to false, or mortgage_los_get_staff with the staff ID if discovered from prior context (or similar), to confirm her employment status. Expected findings: Rachel Kim (los_staff_266153ea2738), role "processor", is_active false, termination_date 2025-06-30, confirming she departed nearly 10 months before the VPN sessions.
OE 9: Search emails using search_emails with query "Rachel Kim" or "Keystone systems" or "credentials" (or similar) to find communications with the former employee. Expected findings: Denise's outreach email (email_inject_vpn_003) to rachel.kim.personal@gmail.com on April 25 asking whether Rachel accessed any Keystone systems since her departure, and Rachel's reply (email_inject_vpn_004) from her personal email denying access, stating she returned her laptop and badge on her last day in June, and asking whether she should be concerned about identity theft.
OE 10: Search QuickBooks vendors using quickbooks_search_vendors (or similar) to find the ghost vendor. Expected findings: vendor "Lakeview Compliance Consulting" (Id: V-LAKEVIEW-001), no phone number, email billing@lakeviewcompliance.net, billing address P.O. Box 4417, Gastonia NC 28054, balance $1,850.
OE 11: Search QuickBooks bills using quickbooks_search_bills (or similar) to find the associated bill. Expected findings: bill BILL-2026-0423-LCC from Lakeview Compliance Consulting, TxnDate 2026-04-23, DueDate 2026-04-30 (Thursday, 2 days from universe date), TotalAmt $1,850, description "Q1 2026 compliance advisory services - engagement letter on file"
OE 12: Search emails using search_emails with query "Lakeview" or "vendor" or "Hana" (or similar) to find the internal discovery chain for the ghost vendor. Expected findings: Hana Kim's email (email_inject_vendor_001) to Victor Osei on April 24 asking about the unknown vendor with a $1,850 bill, Victor's reply (email_inject_vendor_002) denying knowledge and comparing it to "the Premier Data Solutions situation from last month", Hana's forward (email_inject_vendor_003) to Grace Yamamoto (CC Denise) noting neither she nor Victor recognizes it, and Grace's email (email_inject_vendor_004) to Denise on April 25 connecting the VPN credential compromise to the ghost vendor, noting the vendor was created during the April 21 session and warning about auto-pay on the April 30 due date.
OE 13: Search Slack messages using conversations_search_messages with search_query "Lakeview" or "vendor" (or similar), or browse #general (C001) using conversations_history, to find Victor's Slack post. Expected findings: Victor's message in #general (April 24) asking if anyone knows who Lakeview Compliance Consulting is, noting a $1,850 bill due next week with no contract on file.
OE 14: Search CRM engagements using crm_list_engagements or crm_show_data (or similar) to find internal notes documenting the investigation. Expected findings: Hana's engagement note (crm_engagement_inject_lcc_001, April 24) flagging the unrecognized vendor with $1,850 bill details, Grace's engagement note (crm_engagement_inject_lcc_002, April 25) connecting the VPN credential compromise to the ghost vendor, and Denise's engagement note (crm_engagement_inject_refund_001, April 27) documenting the unauthorized $500 Stripe refund on Jessica Kim's appraisal fee and linking it to the credential compromise with combined financial impact.
OE 15: Send an email (via send_email) from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to raj.anand@keystonemortgage.com with a compiled summary of all findings including: Rachel Kim's compromised credentials (terminated June 2025, VPN sessions April 20-22), systems accessed (QuickBooks, Stripe, email gateway), the Lakeview Compliance Consulting ghost vendor ($1,850 bill due April 30), the unauthorized $500 refund on Jessica Kim's appraisal charge (LN-2025-00483), Natasha Okafor's denial of processing the refund, Rachel Kim's denial of accessing Keystone systems, who flagged the vendor internally (Hana Kim, Victor Osei, Grace Yamamoto), and the total financial exposure ($2,350).
OE 16: Send an email (via send_email) from denise.holloway@keystonemortgage.com to natasha.okafor@keystonemortgage.com informing the loan officer about the unauthorized refund on her borrower Jessica Kim's appraisal charge on loan LN-2025-00483, explaining it was tied to a credential compromise, and providing enough context for Natasha to address the situation with the client if needed.
OE 17: Add an activity note to the loan record using mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id for LN-2025-00483, action such as "compliance_flag" or "note" (or similar), and detail describing the unauthorized $500 Stripe refund (re_Kq8RvTm3nWx5pL2d) processed via compromised credentials on April 22, not authorized by the assigned loan officer Natasha Okafor or the borrower Jessica Kim.
OE 18: Post a message in the IT support channel using conversations_add_message with channel_id "C008" and payload summarizing the full scope of the credential compromise: Rachel Kim's VPN sessions (April 20-22), systems accessed (QuickBooks and Stripe), the ghost vendor Lakeview Compliance Consulting ($1,850 bill), the unauthorized $500 refund on Jessica Kim's appraisal charge, the total financial exposure, and the connection between the access sessions and the financial activity.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
